commit.message:
Replace pytz with stdlib zoneinfo across timezone-related code and docs (#41017)

`pytz` is a third-party dependency; Python 3.9+ ships `zoneinfo` in the
standard library as its replacement. This PR removes all `pytz`
references in favour of `zoneinfo`.

## Changes

- **`FacilityInfoTest.py`** — `test_timezones`: swap
`pytz.timezone(name)` → `ZoneInfo(name)` and `str(tz)` → `tz.key` for
the equality assertion
- **`FacilitiesFile.rst`** — update `timezone` attribute docs: link to
`zoneinfo` stdlib docs, reference `zoneinfo.available_timezones()`
instead of `pytz.all_timezones`
- **`FacilityInfo.h`** — update two comments (method doc + member
variable) from "compatible with pytz" → "compatible with zoneinfo"
- **`DateAndTime.rst`** — replace pytz code example and prose with
zoneinfo equivalent

```python
# Before
import pytz
tz = pytz.timezone(ConfigService.getFacility().timezone())
times = numpy.datetime_as_string(times, timezone=tz)

# After
from zoneinfo import ZoneInfo
tz = ZoneInfo(ConfigService.getFacility().timezone())
times = numpy.datetime_as_string(times, timezone=tz)
```

There is no associated issue, but this is related to
[EWM14929](https://ornlrse.clm.ibmcloud.com/ccm/web/projects/Neutron%20Data%20Project%20(Change%20Management)#action=com.ibm.team.workitem.viewWorkItem&id=14929)

### To test:

The examples should continue to work as-is.

commit.message:
refactor(routing): non-Azure deployments respect Settings→LLM DB config

Previously the non-Azure branch (local dev, OVH) completely bypassed the
DB routing config and hardcoded azure-openai for reasoning agents.

Now:
1. Check Settings → LLM DB config first (reasoning_heavy / production_heavy /
   tasks_heavy keys) — lets operators configure a different model per category
   from the UI without touching code.
2. Fall back to PLATFORM_LLM_PROVIDER / PLATFORM_LLM_MODEL env vars.

On OVH (PLATFORM_LLM_PROVIDER=minimax): all agents use minimax by default,
but an operator can set reasoning_heavy → {provider: minimax, model: MiniMax-Text-01}
via Settings → LLM to use a stronger model for architect/reasoning agents.

commit.message:
Convert priority 3 MethodDescCallSite calls to UnmanagedCallersOnly (#124854)

First set of priority 3 conversions from #123864 — replace
`MethodDescCallSite`/`CallDescrWorker` calls with `UnmanagedCallersOnly`
reverse P/Invoke calls.

## Converted call sites

| File | Call site | Method |
|------|-----------|--------|
| `appdomain.cpp` | `AppDomain::RaiseExitProcessEvent` |
`ON_PROCESS_EXIT` |
| `appdomain.cpp` | `AppDomain::OnUnhandledException` |
`ON_UNHANDLED_EXCEPTION` |
| `assembly.cpp` | `RunManagedStartup` | `MANAGED_STARTUP` |
| `assembly.cpp` | `RunMainInternal` (TARGET_BROWSER) |
`HANDLE_ASYNC_ENTRYPOINT` |
| `invokeutil.cpp` | `InvokeUtil::CreateClassLoadExcept` |
`ReflectionTypeLoadException` ctor |
| `invokeutil.cpp` | `InvokeUtil::CreateTargetExcept` |
`TargetInvocationException` ctor |

## Remaining priority 3 items (follow ups)

- Main entry point in `RunMainInternal` (dynamic MethodDesc)
- `CustomAttribute_CreateCustomAttributeInstance` (dynamic ctor, already
a QCall)
- `CorHost2::ExecuteAssembly` (dynamic method lookup)
- `FuncEvalWrapper`/`DoNormalFuncEval` (debugger func-eval)

## Pattern

Follows the same conversion pattern as #124834 (priority 2). Each
conversion adds an `[UnmanagedCallersOnly]` managed wrapper with an
`Exception*` out-parameter and replaces the native `MethodDescCallSite`
+ `ARG_SLOT` with
`UnmanagedCallersOnlyCaller::InvokeThrowing`.

---------

commit.message:
feat: MetadataCompiler — dense struct-of-arrays runtime tables (#1059)

Add MetadataCompiler that converts DataEntityMetadata/DataFieldMetadata into
cache-friendly ordinal-indexed runtime tables at startup:

- EntityTable: struct-of-arrays indexed by contiguous EntityId, sorted by slug
  with binary-search slug→EntityId resolver
- FieldTable: struct-of-arrays indexed by contiguous FieldId, entity-grouped
  with packed FieldFlags and WireFieldType mapping
- RouteTable: flat dispatch array indexed by (entityId << 4) | verbOrdinal
  for O(1) handler resolution
- RuntimeSnapshot: immutable container with atomic Interlocked.Exchange swap
- MetadataCompiler.Compile/CompileAndSwap: assigns contiguous IDs, builds all
  tables, returns frozen snapshot
- Wired into startup (after RuntimeEntityRegistry.Build) and gallery rebuild
- 11 unit tests covering empty/single/multi entity compilation, slug lookup,
  flag packing, wire-type mapping, route dispatch, and atomic swap

Closes #1056

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore(core-xml): bump fast-xml-parser minimum to ^5.3.7 to fix critical CVEs (#37489) `@azure/core-xml` declared `fast-xml-parser: ^5.0.7`, allowing versions 4.1.3–5.3.5 affected by two critical CVEs to satisfy the dependency range. ### Changes - **`sdk/core/core-xml/package.json`**: Raise minimum `fast-xml-parser` from `^5.0.7` → `^5.3.7` - **`sdk/core/core-xml/CHANGELOG.md`**: Document security fix in unreleased `1.5.1` section ### Packages impacted by this PR `@azure/core-xml` ### Issues associated with this PR - [GHSA-jmr7-xgp7-cmfj](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-jmr7-xgp7-cmfj) — DoS via unbounded entity expansion in DOCTYPE - [GHSA-m7jm-9gc2-mpf2](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-m7jm-9gc2-mpf2) — Entity encoding bypass via regex injection in DOCTYPE entity names ### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR `fast-xml-parser` versions 4.1.3–5.3.5 have two critical vulnerabilities. The floating `^5.0.7` range allows any vulnerable 5.x version to be resolved. Raising the floor to `^5.3.7` ensures only patched versions are used. ### What are the possible designs available to address the problem? If there are more than one possible design, why was the one in this PR chosen? Only option: update the minimum version constraint. No API changes required; `fast-xml-parser` 5.3.7 is backwards-compatible. ### Are there test cases added in this PR? _(If not, why?)_ No. This is a dependency version floor bump with no behavioral changes — existing tests provide sufficient coverage. ### Provide a list of related PRs _(if any)_ Prior art: [1.3.4 bump of fast-xml-parser to `^4.2.4`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/blob/main/sdk/core/core-xml/CHANGELOG.md#134-2023-06-20) for a prior security issue. ### Checklists - [x] Added impacted package name to the issue description. - [ ] Does this PR need any fixes in the SDK Generator?** _(If so, create an Issue in the [Autorest/typescript](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript) repository and link it here.)_ - [x] Added a changelog (if necessary).
Original prompt > > ---- > > *This section details on the original issue you should resolve* > > Security vulnerability: fast-xml-parser > It looks like there is a dependency on v5.3.1 of fast-xml-parser: > > > > fast-xml-parser 4.1.3 - 5.3.5 > Severity: critical > fast-xml-parser affected by DoS through entity expansion in DOCTYPE (no expansion limit) - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-jmr7-xgp7-cmfj > fast-xml-parser has an entity encoding bypass via regex injection in DOCTYPE entity names - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-m7jm-9gc2-mpf2 > > Upgrade minimum for fast-xml-parser in core-xml to ^5.3.7 > > ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section) > > > @timovv > Hi @skhilliard, since `@azure/core-xml` takes a floating dependency on `fast-xml-parser@^5.0.7`, it will accept any version 5.0.7 or above and below 6.0.0. We can update the minimum here, but in the meantime, you should be able to resolve this issue immediately by doing a dependency upgrade (`npm update`, etc.), which will allow you to pull in a patched version of `fast-xml-parser`. > > Let us know if you have any questions. > >

commit.message: fix(inventory-app): fix test:mocha not running tests due to Quill accessing document at import time (#26660) ## Summary `pnpm test:mocha` in `inventory-app` is silently failing to run any of its 9 tests (not counted as failures, just absent; see [today](https://dev.azure.com/fluidframework/public/public%20Team/_build/results?buildId=382505&view=logs&j=855df218-f14a-5111-4dba-a3c8eab6872c&t=a5446ac8-4e73-5540-487b-79110ad67165&l=3015) VS [with this PR](https://dev.azure.com/fluidframework/public/public%20Team/_build/results?buildId=382616&view=logs&j=855df218-f14a-5111-4dba-a3c8eab6872c&t=a5446ac8-4e73-5540-487b-79110ad67165&l=3884)). Locally this is what I see: - Root cause: `@fluidframework/react/alpha` re-exports quill-dependent text editor components. Quill's `emitter.js` calls `document.addEventListener()` at the **module's top level**, before any test `before()` hook can set up jsdom. - Fix: add `src/test/mochaHooks.ts` that calls `globalJsdom()` at module level, loaded via Node's `--import` flag (`.mocharc.cjs`) so it runs before mocha evaluates any test files or modules imported (in this case, transitively) by them. This is the same pattern already used by the `text-editor` example package. 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) --------- Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Replace blob redirect URLs with direct raw.githubusercontent.com URLs (#2159)

The crank controller intermittently fails to follow GitHub's 302 redirect
when loading config files from blob URLs with ?raw=true suffix. This causes
CONFIG_LOAD failures in benchmark pipeline runs (e.g. pipeline 1208).

Replacing all github.com/aspnet/Benchmarks/blob/main/...?raw=true URLs with
direct raw.githubusercontent.com/aspnet/Benchmarks/main/... URLs eliminates
the redirect hop and makes config loading more reliable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add FlaUI end-to-end UI automation tests (12/12 passing)

- New TimeGuard.UITests project using FlaUI.Core + FlaUI.UIA3 + xUnit
- AppFixture launches TimeGuard.exe against a throw-away temp SQLite DB
  (TIMEGUARD_TEST_DB env var) so tests never touch the real production DB
- SeededAppFixture pre-seeds password hash so first-run window is skipped
- 12 tests across 4 windows: FirstRun, Settings, Dashboard, BlockedPopup
- AssemblyInfo.cs disables xUnit parallel execution (tests drive a real
  process; parallel fixtures kill each other)
- watch.ps1 file-watcher re-runs UI tests automatically on every .cs/.xaml save
- Fix: PasswordPromptWindow now accepts DatabaseService via constructor
  instead of creating new DatabaseService() (which used the production DB,
  causing password verification to fail against the test DB)
- Fix: App.xaml.cs catches AbandonedMutexException on startup so re-launched
  processes (killed by tests) don't exit immediately on mutex check
- docs: README updated with UI test commands and watch.ps1 usage

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(wasm): use qtHaveModule guards instead of wasm_emscripten QT -=

The wasm_emscripten scope check was not firing for Qt 6.6.3 wasm_singlethread
(mkspec may be 'wasm-emscripten-singlethread' not 'wasm-emscripten').
Replace unconditional QT += concurrent/bluetooth/webenginewidgets with
qtHaveModule() guards so modules are only requested when present, making
the .pro portable across all Qt configurations without needing scope logic.
Also broaden the WASM block condition to covers all mkspec name variants.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add distributed lock for cross-pod concurrent request protection

- Register IConnectionMultiplexer singleton in Program.cs
- Add FailureCode.ConcurrentRequest (HTTP 409) to Failure.cs
- Use Redis SETNX (StringSetAsync When.NotExists) inside GetOrCreateAsync factory
  to ensure only one pod executes the action per idempotency key
- acquiredToken hoisted outside try block so lock is released AFTER
  HybridCache writes to L2, preventing race between lock release and cache write
- Release lock via atomic Lua script (check-and-delete) in finally block
- Add ConcurrentIdempotentRequestException and LockExpiry (30s) constant

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: lazy-load liked posts on ProfileScreen to avoid unnecessary 404

The Likes tab data was eagerly fetched on mount even when the default
Posts tab is active. If the backend doesn't serve /users/:username/likes
(e.g. older deployment), this produced a noisy 404 error on every
profile visit. Now liked posts are only fetched when the user first
switches to the Likes tab.
